patchNotes.headline: Screen sharing fixed, plus chat and session polish
patchNotes.summary: v1.0.43 fixes screen sharing startup on Mac and Windows, keeps app sessions intact across normal restarts, and carries forward the recent messaging, room-history, and UI improvements from the .42 pass.

sections.title: Screen sharing
items: Fixed a startup bug where screen sharing could fail immediately with a getDisplayMedia constraints error on both Mac and Windows.
Screen capture now requests a browser-supported profile first, then tightens quality on the active track after sharing starts.

sections.title: Messaging polish
items: Messages now appear locally right away when you send them in secure DMs and rooms, instead of waiting for the round trip.
Historical room messages that cannot be decrypted are now hidden from the timeline instead of showing scary failure rows.
Opening General or other room history now feels cleaner when older encrypted messages are present.

sections.title: Interface
items: The Messages list in the left sidebar now shows profile pictures when they exist instead of falling back to initials.
Logout now asks for confirmation before ending your session.

sections.title: Session and streaming
items: Normal app restarts and updates no longer force an immediate sign-in again just because the signer is not already back in memory.
Screen sharing now requests a more aggressive high-resolution capture profile before encoding, with stronger bitrate and frame pacing targets.
Streaming is tuned to hold sharper detail and smoother motion more consistently on supported displays.
Windows stays pinned to v1.0.40 until a real Windows-native build is published.
